tagline: any task → the loop it needs

task.title: Fix off-by-one in pagination offset
task.description: last page drops one row; pure function, no side effects

checks.name: compiles
checks.why: the code has to at least build before anything else matters

checks.name: unit-tests
checks.why: behavior is pinned by tests, not vibes

checks.name: repro-test
checks.why: a test that fails on the bug and passes on the fix — otherwise we never proved we fixed it

loop.exit_condition: all required checks pass AND no critic still objects
rationale: task kind is 'bugfix', so the loop centers on a reproduction test as the real exit gate

code.buggy:
def page(items, page_num, size):
    start = page_num * size
    end = start + size - 1          # bug: drops the last row of every page
    return items[start:end]
code.fixed:
def page(items, page_num, size):
    start = page_num * size
    end = start + size              # fixed: slice end is exclusive
    return items[start:end]

tests.unit:
def _run_tests():
    data = list(range(10))
    assert page(data, 0, 3) == [0, 1, 2]
    assert page(data, 1, 3) == [3, 4, 5]

task.description: rotate refresh tokens; touches auth and sessions

task.title: Add SSO token refresh to the login flow

checks.why: the code has to at least build before anything else matters

checks.why: behavior is pinned by tests, not vibes

checks.why: the new behavior is demonstrated end to end

loop.exit_condition: all required checks pass AND no critic still objects
rationale: task kind is 'feature', so the loop centers on an end-to-end acceptance test
touches a high-stakes surface (auth, sessions), so a human gate is proposed and a security critic should be recruited

payments: a retried payment must not double-charge
billing: a retried charge must not double-bill
db: no orphaned or corrupted rows after the change
schema: the schema change preserves existing rows
concurrency: no lost updates under concurrent access
